name: United Bank of Philadelphia
description_short: Independent Philadelphia-based bank offering personal and business banking, online services, and small business lending with 30+ years of local market expertise.
description_long: United Bank of Philadelphia is an independent, FDIC-insured bank headquartered in Philadelphia that has served the greater Philadelphia area for over 30 years. The bank emphasizes its local roots and independence, positioning itself as one of the few remaining independent banks still headquartered in Philadelphia, distinct from larger Wall Street-focused institutions. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal and business checking/savings accounts, online and mobile banking platforms, bill pay services, loan products (personal and business), merchant services through Clover payment processing, wire transfers, safe deposit boxes, ATM access, and remote deposit capture. They provide both retail and business banking solutions with dedicated online banking portals for each segment, including a specialized eCorp platform for business customers. United Bank of Philadelphia distinguishes itself through its stated commitment to community investment and neighborhood revitalization in Philadelphia. The bank highlights its role in funding projects that create jobs and revitalize communities, offers financial education initiatives, and operates a Small Business Resource Center. Their marketing emphasizes understanding local market opportunities and challenges specific to Philadelphia, framing banking as embedded in community success rather than purely transactional. As a traditional community bank, United Bank of Philadelphia serves customers seeking local banking relationships and community-focused financial services. The bank appears well-established and stable, though independent banks typically face resource constraints relative to larger institutions regarding technology innovation and service breadth.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: FDIC-insured with backing by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
30+ years of established presence and reputation in Philadelphia market
Independent bank headquartered in Philadelphia, not subject to Wall Street institutional priorities
Comprehensive digital banking: online banking, mobile banking with remote deposit capture, and 24/7 bill pay
Dedicated small business support including Small Business Resource Center with free guides
Merchant services integration through Clover platform with quarterly incentive pricing
Multiple service channels including telephone banking, night depository, and bank by mail
cons: Limited geographic footprint as regional Philadelphia bank may restrict access for non-local customers
Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, loan terms, or fee structures for comparison
No information provided about minimum account balance requirements or monthly maintenance fees
As independent community bank, likely has fewer technology resources than national banks or fintech competitors
Business loan and personal loan details are vague; no specifics on loan amounts, rates, or eligibility criteria
best_for: Philadelphia-area residents and small business owners seeking local banking relationships
Entrepreneurs and small businesses prioritizing community bank support over national chain banking
Customers valuing established local bank stability and community investment commitment
Businesses using point-of-sale systems interested in Clover merchant services integration
diagnosis: United Bank of Philadelphia is best for Philadelphia-area customers and small business owners seeking established local banking with community investment focus. The main caveat is that specific pricing, rates, and fee information are not disclosed on their website, making it difficult to assess competitive positioning without direct contact; customers should compare their offerings against national and regional banks before committing.
services: Personal and business checking and savings accounts
Online banking with 18-month transaction history and 24/7 fund transfers
Mobile banking with check deposit via photo capture
Bill pay (free standard, same-day and overnight options available)
Merchant services through Clover payment processing platform
Telephone banking and bank by mail services
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1501 N Broad St suite 17, Philadelphia, PA 19122
